zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Scott, Knight of Kent, sword-bearer to Henry V at Agincourt於1390年出生在英國英格蘭肯特斯可也斯 霍爾時，他的父親John Scott I20歲，母親Matilda de la Cumbe20歲。他於1409年之前與Joane Orlastone結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1430年被列為member of parliament for kent。他去世於1434年2月5日在英國肯特布拉布恩享年44歲，埋葬在英國肯特布拉布恩。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William Scott, Knight of Kent, sword-bearer to Henry V at Agincourt于1390年出生在英国英格兰肯特斯可也斯 霍爾时，他的父亲John Scott I20岁，母亲Matilda de la Cumbe20岁。他于1409年之前与Joane Orlastone结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他于1430年被列为member of parliament for kent。他去世于1434年2月5日在英国肯特布拉布恩享年44岁，埋葬在英国肯特布拉布恩。

nameMeanings.name: Scott,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (Down): habitational and ethnic name from Middle English Scot ‘man from Scotland’. There is no evidence that the surname denoted either of the earlier senses of Scot as ‘(Gaelic-speaking) Irishman’ or ‘man from Alba’, the Gaelic-speaking region of Scotland north of the river Forth. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
English and Scottish: from the rare Middle English personal name Scot (Old English Scott, possibly also Old Norse Skotr), only certainly attested in northern England.
English: variant of   Scutt  .

variants.name: Scutt
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Pierre.
Americanized form of Dutch   Schutte   ‘archer’.
English: nickname for a short person, from Middle English scut(e) ‘short’, or else for a swift runner, from Middle English scut ‘hare’.

nameMeanings.name: Knight
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status or occupational name from Middle English knight ‘retainer, attendant’ (Old English cniht ‘boy, youth, lad)’. The specialized feudal sense ‘a high-ranking tenant bound to serve his lord as a mounted soldier’ is not known to have ever given rise to the surname, although it is not out of the question that it may occasionally have been used as a nickname, perhaps for someone who played the part of an armed knight in a local pageant.
Irish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the knight’. See also McKnight .

variants.name: Knights
meanings: English (East Anglia): variant of Knight , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McKnight
meanings: Irish: part translation of Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the horseman (Gaelic ridire)’, formerly Anglicized McRuddery.
Scottish (Dumfriesshire and Galloway) and Irish (Ulster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Neachtain, which is usually Anglicized as McNaughton .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Kent,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from the county of Kent. The surname is also established in Ireland, where English bearers of this name settled in Meath, in the 13th century. The name has been Gaelicized as Ceannt.

nameMeanings.name: to
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Minh, Thanh, Dung, Vinh, Hung, Son, Chuong, Ha, Hien, Hoa, Huong, Mai.  Chinese Wai, Chi, Hang, Hon, Lai, Sang, Sze, Chan, Hing, Man Ching, Chen, Cheung.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 塗 (or 涂) and 屠, see   Tu   1 and 2.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 陶, see   Tao   1.

variants.name: Tu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Muoi, Anh, Dung, Hung, Minh, Thanh, Hue, Tuan, Ha, Hoa, Lien, Linh.  Chinese Chan, Chi, Hong, Qiang, Weiming, Cheng, Chu, Chuan, Hon, Jin, Liwei, Ping, Chung, Thai, Tuong, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Chang Yol, Min, Shen, Tian, Uyen.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 塗 (or 涂), meaning ‘paint, smear’ in Chinese: (i) from Tu (涂), the ancient name of the Chu river (located in present-day Anhui province and Jiangsu province). (ii) borne by descendants of Tu Qin (塗欽 or 涂欽) (246–337  AD ), an official during the Western and Eastern Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ), who migrated to southern China.（iii) shortened form of the compound surname Tu-Shan (塗山 or 涂山).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 屠, meaning ‘slaughter’ in Chinese: (i) shortened form of the compound surname Zou-Tu (鄒屠), the name of an ancient place (possibly located in Shandong province). (ii) simplified form of the placename Tu (屠阝), the name of a fief (located in Shaanxi province), which was said to be one of the families surnames, also written as Tu (荼) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). (iii) from Tu Peng (屠剻), post name of officials in charge of slaughtering animals in the state of Jin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, French, Walloon, and West Indian (mainly Jamaica and Haiti): from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements haim, heim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’, introduced to England by the Normans in the form Henri. During the Middle Ages this name became enormously popular in England and was borne by eight kings. Continental forms of the personal name were equally popular throughout Europe. In the period in which the majority of English surnames were formed, a common English vernacular form of the name was Harry, hence the surnames Harris (southern) and Harrison (northern). Official documents of the period normally used the Latinized form Henricus. In medieval times, English Henry absorbed an originally distinct Old English personal name that had hagan ‘hawthorn’ (compare Hain 2) as its first element, and there has also been confusion with Amery. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. German Heinrich , and also their derivatives, e.g. Swedish Henriksson (see Henrikson ). Compare Henri .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hInnéirghe ‘descendant of Innéirghe’, a byname based on éirghe ‘arising’.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Éinrí or Mac Einri, patronymics from the personal names Éinrí, Einri, Irish forms of Henry. It is also found as a variant of McEnery .

variants.name: Guccio
meanings: Italian: from the late medieval personal name Guccio, a shortened pet form of any of various names ending in -go, for example Arrigo (see Henry ) or Ugo (see Hugh ).

variants.name: Arrigo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Antonio, Pasquale, Salvatore, Sal, Santo, Vincenzo, Antonino, Franco, Mario, Natale, Vito.
Italian: from the medieval personal name Arrigo, a variant of Enrico (see Henry ).

variants.name: Arch
meanings: English: topographic name for someone living by a bridge, from Middle English, Old French arche ‘arch’ (i.e. of a bridge).
English (of Norman origin): shortened form of Arches, a habitational name from any of various places in France called Arques (Eure, Pas-de-Calais, Seine-Maritime).
Germanized form of Slovenian Arh: from a shortened form of the personal name Areh, an old vernacular form of Henrik (see Henry 1).

variants.name: Harris
meanings: English (southern England and south Wales): from the personal name Harry + genitival -s. This surname is also established in Ireland, taken there principally during the Plantation of Ulster. However, in some cases, particularly in families coming from County Mayo, Harris can be an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hEarchadha. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

American shortened and altered form of Greek surnames begining with Cha(r)-, such as Chasandrinos (variant of Kassandrinos, a habitational name from the Kassandra peninsula of Chalkidiki), and various patronymics from the personal name Charalampos (see Charos ). In North America, the surname Harris may possibly also originate from a transferred use of the Greek personal (given) name Charis or Harris (shortened forms of Charalampos) as a surname (i.e. as a replacement of the original surname).
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames.

variants.name: Enrique
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Santiago, Julio, Nelida, Raul, Alberto, Alejandro, Alejo, Alicia, Amalia, Andres.
Spanish: from the personal name Enrique, an equivalent of Henry .

variants.name: Endrizzi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Bruna, Ezio, Marino.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of a pet form of Endrigo, a variant of Enrico, Italian form of Henry .

variants.name: Enrico
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Domingo, Leoncio, Luis, Manuel, Melquiades, Nestor.  Italian Alberto, Aldo, Domenic, Gino, Livio, Pio.
Italian: from the personal name Enrico, Italian form of Henry 1.

variants.name: D'Errico
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Domenic, Salvatore, Angelo, Dante, Donato, Elio, Giampaolo, Giovanni, Marco, Matteo, Mauro.
Italian: patronymic from the personal name Errico, an equivalent of Henry . Compare Derico .

variants.name: Guzzo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Angelo, Salvatore, Agostino, Carmelina, Domenic, Gildo, Giorgio, Nunzio, Santo, Saverio.
Italian:
from a late medieval personal name Guzzo, a shortened pet form of various personal names, for example Arriguzzo, from Arrigo (see Henry ), and Uguzzo, from Ugo (see Hugh ).

variants.name: Harriott
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Donal, Donovan.
English (Sussex):
from the medieval personal names Henriot and Herriot, pet forms of Henry and Herry (see Harry ).

nameMeanings.name: at
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch: from a short form of the personal name Adelhard, which is of ancient Germanic origin, composed of the elements adal ‘noble’ + hard ‘bold, hardy’. Compare Ale and Alma 4.
Swedish: variant of   Ahl  .

variants.name: Ale
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos Enrique, Cesar, Jose, Maria De Jesus.
Spanish (Andalusia; Alé): unexplained.
Italian (Sicily; Alé): probably a variant of Aleo .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Scott, Knight of Kent, sword-bearer to Henry V at Agincourt was born in 1390, in Scot's Hall, Kent, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Scott I, was 20 and his mother, Matilda de la Cumbe, was 20. He married Joane Orlastone before 1409. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. In 1430, at the age of 40, his occupation is listed as member of parliament for kent. He died on 5 February 1434, in Brabourne, Kent, England, at the age of 44, and was buried in Brabourne, Kent, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Scott, Knight of Kent, sword-bearer to Henry V at Agincourt est né en 1390, à Scot's Hall, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Scott I, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Matilda de la Cumbe, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Joane Orlastone avant 1409. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. En 1430, à l’âge de 40 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est member of parliament for kent. Il est décédé le 5 février 1434, à Brabourne, Kent, Angleterre, à l’âge de 44 ans, et a été enterré à Brabourne, Kent, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William Scott, Knight of Kent, sword-bearer to Henry V at Agincourt 1390 in Scot's Hall, Kent, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Scott I 20 und seine Mutter Matilda de la Cumbe 20. Er hat vor 1409 Joane Orlastone geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. 1430, als er 40 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als member of parliament for kent aufgeführt. Er starb am 5. Februar 1434 im Alter von 44 Jahren in Brabourne, Kent, England und wurde in Brabourne, Kent, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Scott, Knight of Kent, sword-bearer to Henry V at Agincourt è nato il nel 1390 nel Scot's Hall, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, John Scott I, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Matilda de la Cumbe, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Joane Orlastone prima del 1409. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Nel 1430, all'età di 40 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come member of parliament for kent. Morì il 5 febbraio 1434, nel Brabourne, Kent, England, all'età di 44 anni, e fu sepolto a Brabourne, Kent, England.

ja.Brabourne, Kent, England: イギリスケントブラボウルン
ja.lifeSummary: Sir William Scott, Knight of Kent, sword-bearer to Henry V at Agincourtが1390年にイギリスイギリスケントスコト'ス ハルで生まれた時，父John Scott Iは20歳，母Matilda de la Cumbeは20歳であった。1409年以前にJoane Orlastoneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。1430年，40歳の時，職業はmember of parliament for kentと記録されている。彼は1434年2月5日にイギリスケントブラボウルンで44歳で死去し，イギリスケントブラボウルンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Scott, Knight of Kent, sword-bearer to Henry V at Agincourt(이)가 1390년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 스컷'스 홀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Scott I(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda de la Cumbe(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1409년 이전 Joane Orlastone(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1430년, 40세의 나이로 그의 직업은 member of parliament for kent이었다. 그는 1434년 2월 5일 영국 켄트 브라보우른에서 44세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 켄트 브라보우른에 묻혔다.

pt.Scots Hall, Brabourne, Kent, England: Scot's Hall, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Scott, Knight of Kent, sword-bearer to Henry V at Agincourt nasceu em 1390, em Scot's Hall, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John Scott I, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Matilda de la Cumbe, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Joane Orlastone antes 1409. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Em 1430, com 40 anos, sua profissão aparece como member of parliament for kent. Ele faleceu em 5 de fevereiro de 1434, em Brabourne, Kent, Inglaterra, com 44 anos, e foi sepultado em Brabourne, Kent, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Scott, Knight of Kent, sword-bearer to Henry V at Agincourt родился в 1390 г. в местности скот'с холл, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John Scott I было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Matilda de la Cumbe, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Joane Orlastone перед 1409 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. В 1430 году, в возрасте 40 лет,  он работал по профессии member of parliament for kent. Он умер 5 февраля 1434 г. в местности брабурн, кэнт, Англия в возрасте 44 лет и был похоронен в местности брабурн, кэнт, Англия.

es.Scotts Hall, Smeeth, Kent, England: Scot's Hall, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.Scots Hall, Brabourne, Kent, England: Scot's Hall, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William Scott, Knight of Kent, sword-bearer to Henry V at Agincourt nació en 1390, en Scot's Hall, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John Scott I, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Matilda de la Cumbe, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Joane Orlastone antes de 1409. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. En 1430, a la edad de 40 años, su ocupación registrada era member of parliament for kent. Murió el 5 de febrero de 1434, en Brabourne, Kent, Inglaterra, a la edad de 44 años, y fue sepultado en Brabourne, Kent, Inglaterra.
